name: Mushroom Cashew Bisque
recipeInstructions:
1. To make cashew creme: Puree 1/2 cup raw cashews and 1/4 cup water in a blender or food processor. Gradually add another 1/4 cup water, blending until smooth.
2. To make bisque: Rinse dried shiitake mushrooms and soak them in one cup of hot stock or water to create broth. Drain the liquid and set aside. Finely chop the rehydrated mushrooms.
3. In a pot, sauté diced onions, carrots, and celery in olive oil and sesame oil over medium heat until lightly browned.
4. Add minced garlic, fresh and rehydrated mushrooms, and 2 tablespoons of the reserved mushroom liquid to the pot. Cook until the mushrooms are soft, about 3 to 5 minutes.
5. Stir in sherry wine, bay leaf, black pepper, soy sauce, chopped parsley, remaining mushroom liquid, water, and hot vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Remove from heat and let the soup cool to room temperature. Whisk together flour and the reserved liquid until smooth. Stir this mixture into the soup, adding cashew butter and cashew creme. Cook over low heat for 5 minutes or until slightly thickened.
Per serving: 232 calories; 8 grams protein; 300 milligrams sodium; 35 grams carbohydrates; 10 grams fat; 0 milligrams cholesterol; 203 calories
